description: Sanity Migration Phase 2: Fix award titles and formFields schema, add presentation tool with visual editing, upload 3D/media assets to Sanity CDN, add missing schemas for scenes/inspectables/physicsParams

userStories.title: Fix award titles in migration script
userStories.description: As a content editor, I want award titles in Sanity to show the actual award name (e.g. 'Awwwards Site of the Day') so that when we switch the frontend to Sanity, awards display correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: In `scripts/migration/fix-awards.ts`, add `title: true` to the BaseHub query (alongside `_title: true`)
Change line 106 from `title: award._title` to `title: award.title || award._title`
Re-run the migration: `pnpm exec tsx scripts/migration/fix-awards.ts`
GROQ query `*[_type == "award"]{title}` returns names like 'Awwwards Site of the Day', 'FWA Site of the Day', 'Webby Award' -- not internal slugs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Root cause: fix-awards.ts:106 uses award._title (BaseHub internal name) instead of award.title (the SELECT field with actual award names). The BaseHub query never fetches `title: true`.

userStories.title: Fix open position formFields schema
userStories.description: As a content editor, I want the form fields configuration for open positions to be a multi-select list in Sanity Studio so I can pick which fields appear on each job application form.
acceptanceCriteria: In `sanity/schemas/documents/openPosition.ts`, change `formFields` from `type: 'string'` to `type: 'array'` of strings with `options.list` containing all 12 predefined values

Predefined options: 'First and last name', 'Email', 'Where are you based', 'Why do you want to join', 'Years of experience', 'Skills', 'Portfolio', 'Github', 'Availability to start', 'Availability to work', 'Linkedin', 'Salary Expectation'
Re-run careers migration: `pnpm exec tsx scripts/migration/migrate-careers.ts`
GROQ query `*[_type == "openPosition"]{title, "fields": applyFormSetup.formFields}` returns `fields` as an array of strings, not a single string
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BaseHub stores formFields as BSHBSelect_944951315[] (array of select values). Sanity schema has it as plain string. Frontend expects string[] and calls .includes() on it.

userStories.title: Add presentation tool to Sanity Studio
userStories.description: As a content editor, I want a Presentation tab in Sanity Studio that shows a live preview of the website so I can see content changes in context.
acceptanceCriteria: Import `presentationTool` from `sanity/presentation` in `sanity.config.ts`
Add `presentationTool({ previewUrl: { previewMode: { enable: '/api/draft-mode/enable' } } })` to the plugins array
'Presentation' tab appears in Sanity Studio alongside 'Structure' and 'Vision'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: sanity v5.18+ exports presentationTool from sanity/presentation -- no separate package install needed.

userStories.title: Set up draft mode API routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need draft mode enable/disable API routes so the presentation tool can toggle Next.js draft mode for live previews.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/app/api/draft-mode/enable/route.ts`: GET handler that enables draft mode via `(await draftMode()).enable()` and redirects to `searchParams.get('redirect') || '/'`
Create `src/app/api/draft-mode/disable/route.ts`: GET handler that disables draft mode and returns JSON response
Draft mode enable endpoint validates a shared secret token from query params or env var to prevent unauthorized access
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Next.js 16+ requires awaiting draftMode(): `(await draftMode()).enable()`. Use SANITY_VIEWER_TOKEN or similar env var for secret validation.

userStories.title: Update sanityFetch to support draft perspective
userStories.description: As a developer, I need sanityFetch to switch between published and draft perspectives so visual editing can show unpublished changes.
acceptanceCriteria: In `src/service/sanity/index.ts`, update `sanityFetch` to check draft mode status via `(await draftMode()).isEnabled`
When draft mode is on: use `perspective: 'previewDrafts'`, disable CDN (`useCdn: false`), use a Sanity API token from env
When draft mode is off: keep current behavior (`useCdn: true`, no token, published perspective)
Add a `SANITY_API_READ_TOKEN` env var for authenticated draft queries (document this in .env.example or similar)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current sanityFetch at src/service/sanity/index.ts uses useCdn: true and has no draft support. The token is needed because draft content requires authenticated requests.

userStories.title: Add VisualEditing component for live overlays
userStories.description: As a content editor, I want clickable overlays on page elements in the presentation preview so I can click content and jump to editing it in Studio.
acceptanceCriteria: `VisualEditing` component from `next-sanity` added to the `(site)` group layout, conditionally rendered only when `(await draftMode()).isEnabled` is true
`SanityLive` or `defineLive` from `next-sanity` configured for real-time content updates in preview mode
When in presentation mode, hovering over Sanity-sourced content shows edit overlays
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: next-sanity v12.1+ exports VisualEditing and defineLive. Only add to (site) group layout, not root layout (root also contains /studio).

userStories.title: Create file upload migration utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable utility function that downloads a file from a URL and uploads it to Sanity's asset storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/migration/utils/files.ts` with `downloadAndUploadFile(url: string | undefined | null, filename?: string)` function
Downloads file from external URL into a buffer using fetch
Uploads buffer to Sanity via `sanityWriteClient.assets.upload('file', buffer, { filename })`
Returns `{ _type: 'file', asset: { _type: 'reference', _ref: assetId } }` on success
Returns `null` for empty/undefined/null URLs without throwing
Logs progress and errors per file
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the pattern from existing `scripts/migration/utils/images.ts` (downloadAndUploadImage). Import sanityWriteClient from `scripts/migration/sanity-client.ts`.

userStories.title: Update threeDAssets schema: top-level model fields to file type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the top-level 3D model fields in threeDAssets to use native file type instead of url type.
acceptanceCriteria: In `sanity/schemas/singletons/threeDAssets.ts`, change these top-level fields from `type: 'url'` to `type: 'file'`: officeItems, office, officeWireframe, outdoor, godrays, basketball, basketballNet, contactPhone, routingElements, outdoorCars
Schema deploys without errors (Sanity Studio loads cleanly)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Start with top-level model fields only. Nested objects (sfx, arcade, etc.) are handled in subsequent stories.

userStories.title: Update threeDAssets schema: nested objects to file type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all nested asset fields in threeDAssets (specialEvents, bakes, matcaps, glassReflexes, arcade, characters, pets, lamp, mapTextures, videos) converted to file type.
acceptanceCriteria: Change all `type: 'url'` fields inside nested objects to `type: 'file'`: specialEvents.christmas.tree, specialEvents.christmas.song, bakes[].lightmap, bakes[].ambientOcclusion, matcaps[].file, glassReflexes[].url, arcade.* (all url fields), characters.* (model + textures), pets.* (model + textures), lamp.extraLightmap, mapTextures.rain, mapTextures.basketballVa, videos[].url
Schema deploys without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This covers all nested URL fields except sfx (handled in US-010).

userStories.title: Update threeDAssets schema: sfx fields to file type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all SFX audio fields in threeDAssets converted to file type.
acceptanceCriteria: Change all `type: 'url'` fields inside `sfx` object to `type: 'file'`: basketballTheme, basketballSwoosh, basketballNet, basketballThump, basketballBuzzer, basketballStreak, knobTurning, antenna, blog.lockedDoor items, blog.door[].open/close, blog.lamp[].pull/release, arcade.buttons[].press/release, arcade.sticks[].press/release, arcade.miamiHeatwave, music.aqua/rain/tiger/vhs, contact.interference
Schema deploys without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: sfx is deeply nested with many url fields -- separated into its own story for manageable scope.

userStories.title: Migrate top-level and nested 3D assets to Sanity file storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a migration script that downloads all 3D model, texture, video, and image URLs from threeDAssets and re-uploads them as native Sanity file assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `scripts/migration/migrate-3d-assets-to-files.ts`
Fetches current `threeDAssets` document from Sanity to get all existing URL values
Downloads and uploads each file using `downloadAndUploadFile` from US-007
Processes all sections: top-level models, specialEvents, bakes, matcaps, glassReflexes, arcade, characters, pets, lamp, mapTextures, videos
Builds replacement document with file references and uses `createOrReplace`
Includes rate limiting (100ms delay between uploads or max 3 concurrent)
Continues on individual failures, logs which fields failed
Prints summary: total attempted, succeeded, failed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Does NOT include sfx fields -- those are in US-012. Approximately 40-50 files to upload in this batch.

userStories.title: Migrate SFX audio assets to Sanity file storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SFX audio files migrated to Sanity file storage separately from the main 3D assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend or create migration script to process all sfx fields from threeDAssets

Downloads and uploads all sfx audio URLs: basketball sounds, blog sounds (lockedDoor, door, lamp), arcade sounds (buttons, sticks, miamiHeatwave), music tracks (aqua, rain, tiger, vhs), contact.interference, knobTurning, antenna
Updates the threeDAssets document sfx section with file references
Continues on individual failures with logging
GROQ query resolves sfx file references to valid CDN URLs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Separated from US-011 because sfx has deeply nested arrays and many fields. Approximately 20-30 audio files.

userStories.title: Add scenes schema and migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need scenes data (camera configs, tabs, postprocessing) stored in Sanity so it can eventually replace the BaseHub source.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `scenes` field to `threeDAssets` schema as an array of objects

Each scene object has: `name` (string), `cameraConfig` (object: posX/posY/posZ/tarX/tarY/tarZ/fov/targetScrollY/offsetMultiplier as numbers), `tabs` (array: tabName/tabRoute/tabHoverName/tabClickableName strings + plusShapeScale number), `postprocessing` (object: contrast/brightness/exposure/gamma/vignetteRadius/vignetteSpread/bloomStrength/bloomRadius/bloomThreshold as numbers)
Create migration script that fetches scenes from BaseHub (`threeDInteractions.scenes.scenes.items`) and writes to Sanity
GROQ query returns scenes data matching the `AssetsResult['scenes']` shape from `src/components/assets-provider/fetch-assets.ts`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Scenes come from BaseHub `threeDInteractions.scenes.scenes.items`. No file uploads needed -- just structured data (numbers, strings).

userStories.title: Add inspectables schema and migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need inspectable objects data stored in Sanity so it can eventually replace the BaseHub source.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `inspectables` field to `threeDAssets` schema as an array of objects

Each inspectable has: title (string), specs (array of {title: string, value: string}), description (array of blocks -- portable text), mesh (string), xOffset/yOffset/xRotationOffset/sizeTarget (numbers), scenes (array of strings), fx (file)
Create migration script that fetches inspectables from BaseHub (`pages.inspectables.inspectableList.items`) and writes to Sanity
fx fields uploaded as Sanity file assets using downloadAndUploadFile utility
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Inspectables come from BaseHub `pages.inspectables`. Description is rich text in BaseHub -- use convertRichText utility from scripts/migration/utils/rich-text.ts.

userStories.title: Add physicsParams, glassMaterials, and doubleSideElements
userStories.description: As a developer, I need physicsParams, glassMaterials, and doubleSideElements data in Sanity.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `physicsParams` field to `threeDAssets` schema as array of objects with `title` (string) and `value` (number)
Add `glassMaterials` field as array of strings
Add `doubleSideElements` field as array of strings
Create migration script that fetches these from BaseHub and stores in Sanity
GROQ queries return data matching the shapes in `AssetsResult`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are simple data fields with no file uploads. physicsParams from threeDInteractions.physicsParams, glassMaterials from threeDInteractions.map.glassMaterials, doubleSideElements from threeDInteractions.map.doubleSideElements.

userStories.title: Verify all migrations and deploy schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify that all migrated data is correct and schemas deploy cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: GROQ query `*[_type == "award"]{title}` returns proper award names for all documents
GROQ query `*[_type == "openPosition"]{title, "fields": applyFormSetup.formFields}` returns fields as arrays
GROQ query `*[_type == "threeDAssets"][0]{"officeUrl": office.asset->url}` resolves to a valid CDN URL
GROQ query `*[_type == "threeDAssets"][0]{"sceneCount": count(scenes)}` returns the expected number of scenes
Sanity Studio loads without schema errors
Presentation tab shows website in iframe
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Final verification story. Run all GROQ queries via Sanity Vision tool or sanity CLI.
